title: aralia racemosa subsp. bicrenata
intro: Aralia racemosa subsp. bicrenata, also known as the American spikenard or wild sarsaparilla, is a plant species native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and has been used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Aralia racemosa subsp. bicrenata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
